subtitle: Three absent staff, blocked cases, urgent reference due
context: Kevin, you have 77 unread emails with three critical absences blocking work: Michael O'Sullivan and Emma are both away during the week commencing 1 July 2026 per Julie Hickman's update, and their absence impacts OSM Change Enablement approvals which Sarah Warne has confirmed were temporarily reversed as of 23 June. The DSE System change request from James Salas Guillen to exclude SBS users (referenced in Tony Boydell's CR draft at Azure workitem 126017) is awaiting your review and approval. Case 69001655 with Access Support has been waiting 48+ hours since 23 June without resolution despite your escalation sent on 19 June to Sarah Murphy - this scheduled IM URL change was supposed to execute today (24 June). The Cority Employee Import process for OxfordUniversity failed on 22 June 17:15 due to SFTP connectivity issues, blocking the production migration that James Salas Guillen flagged. An Urgent Reference Request for Nikolas Sharkey arrived on 23 June at 12:11 from TSS Enquiries and requires your immediate response. Your most time-critical deadline is tomorrow 25 June when the People Conference begins - open first the Reference Request from TSS Enquiries as it flags a time-sensitive employment verification.

prioritiesToday.text: Web pages and URL linking -- job posting updates
prioritiesToday.description: Internal Mobility Principles page moved to a new OxIntranet URL. Three pages needed link updates: main ops intranet page, professional services search page, and individual vacancy application pages. New URL confirmed live on 3 June. Old page retired 23/24 June. E-Form save bug in Search Appointments V4 was blocking updates on vacancy application pages -- Maura McGlynn (AG) applied a config fix on 23 June.
actions: [03 Jun 2026] Meeting with Laura Porter -- reviewed three pages needing link updates.
[22 Jun 2026] URL change approach confirmed working (per Laura 20 Jun).

[23 Jun 2026] Urgent escalation -- unable to save changes in Search Appointments V4 E-Form Maintenance (case 69001555). Kevin requested urgent Teams call with Maura McGlynn (AG). Escalated to Sarah Murphy.
[23 Jun 2026] Maura McGlynn applied a config fix -- reports changes now saving successfully. Kevin asked to review and confirm. (case: 69001555)
[AWAITING] Kevin to review Maura's config fix and confirm E-Form save issue resolved in Search Appointments V4.
[TODO] Complete remaining URL updates on vacancy application pages once E-Form save confirmed working.

prioritiesToday.text: Allocate remaining flex points before 29 June expiry
prioritiesToday.description: Kevin's flex points expire by 29 June 2026. Chemistry bulk deletion (£525) is already committed. Holiday Records Reports quote in progress (case #69001638). Remaining points need to be allocated before expiry.
actions: [20 Jun 2026] Ann-Marie Topliss confirmed FlexPoints case #69001638 formally transitioned to Access Group and progressing to quote stage.
[23 Jun 2026] Kevin escalated case #69001655 to Sarah Murphy requesting assistance with resolution.
[AWAITING] Quote from AG for Holiday Records Reports (case #69001638).
[TODO] Confirm total committed spend and remaining balance once quote received.
[TODO] Identify any further flex point uses before 29 June expiry.

prioritiesToday.text: Pay Code 121 / PAYPAY25 -- add to HR Reporting SQL
prioritiesToday.description: Pay Code 121 (report reference PAYPAY25) needs to be added to the SQL in HR Reporting. Raised by Michael in 1-1 on 15 June -- needed for this month's payroll run. Process: identify which .RDL report(s) handle pay code filtering, edit SQL, render in Visual Studio, deploy DEV -> TEST -> QA -> LIVE.
actions: [15 Jun 2026] Action raised by Michael in 1-1 -- Pay Code 121 / PAYPAY25 missing from SQL report suite. Needed for this month's payroll.
[TODO] Confirm with Michael which specific report(s) in the suite need amending.
[TODO] Edit SQL to add Pay Code 121 / PAYPAY25 to the relevant report(s).
[TODO] Render updated report(s) in Visual Studio and deploy DEV -> TEST -> QA -> LIVE.
[TODO] Verify output in LIVE environment before payroll run.

actions: [23 Jun 2026] Task raised by Kevin -- send POC meeting notes to Chris as priority today.
[TODO] Send POC meeting notes to Christopher Sanders.

prioritiesToday.description: Support Call# 11688462 concerns the Ash VEA team. Multiple emails received 22 June from Marie Cooksey and Anna Carter-Windle. Kevin is named in the emails and a response is expected. Inbox AI triage incorrectly classified as FYI based on subject line alone without reading the body.

[23 Jun 2026] Task created manually -- inbox triage had incorrectly classified as FYI.

prioritiesToday.description: TSS Enquiries (Temporary Staffing Service) has requested an urgent reference from Kevin for Nikolas Sharkey, who is being considered for temporary vacancies at the University of Oxford. Kevin is listed as a referee. Received 23 June at 12:11. Now over 48 hours without a response -- employment verification references are time-critical and can block the candidate's placement.
actions: [23 Jun 2026] Reference request received from TSS Enquiries for Nikolas Sharkey -- 48+ hours outstanding.
[TODO] Open email, complete and return reference for Nikolas Sharkey to TSS Enquiries immediately.

prioritiesToday.description: Julie Hickman confirmed on 23 June that both Michael O'Sullivan AND Emma are on leave during the week commencing 1 July 2026. This leaves no first-line HR Systems support cover for that week. Kevin needs to decide who covers, what can be deferred, and whether Simon Burford or Marie Cooksey need to be informed or involved. The week starts in 8 days -- anyone covering needs adequate notice.
actions: [23 Jun 2026] Julie Hickman confirmed Michael AND Emma both absent w/c 1 July. No cover currently in place.
[TODO] Identify who can provide first-line support cover w/c 1 July.
[TODO] Notify Simon Burford and/or Marie Cooksey of the gap.
[TODO] Confirm cover arrangements and communicate to team.

prioritiesTomorrow.text: Chemistry bulk delete -- get details from Julie, pass to Reenu
prioritiesTomorrow.description: Bulk deletion of appraisal cycle assignments in PeopleXD for Chemistry department. Appraisal cycle name: '(DO NOT USE) CDR2025Chemistry'. 5,568 staff incorrectly assigned and need to be removed. AG contact: Reenu Delaney (cases 68974493 / 67938206). Payment via flex points (£525, approved by Marie). Julie Hickman has run the affected employee report and will share the export via OneDrive.
actions: [Oct 2025] Case 67938206 raised -- Chemistry bulk delete of appraisal assignments.
[Apr 2026] Flex points agreed as payment route -- case 68974493 raised.
[02 Jun 2026] Marie handed case to Kevin as lead.
[03 Jun 2026] Reenu Delaney requested appraisal cycle name and affected employee list.
[09 Jun 2026] Email sent to Julie Hickman requesting details.
[09 Jun 2026] Julie confirmed: cycle name '(DO NOT USE) CDR2025Chemistry', 5,568 staff to be removed. Report run by Julie -- OneDrive export incoming.
[10 Jun 2026] Julie shared OneDrive export of affected employee list — received by Kevin.
[10 Jun 2026] Forwarded cycle name and employee list export to Reenu Delaney at AG.
[10 Jun 2026] Replied to Julie confirming details received and next steps.
[23 Jun 2026] Kevin signed off UAT — changes confirmed good, instructed Reenu to proceed to live. Awaiting confirmation email when live deployment complete. (case: 68974493)
[AWAITING] Reenu Delaney to confirm live deployment complete and email Kevin.

prioritiesTomorrow.description: 2,935 flex points remaining with expiry approximately early July 2026. Simon and Marie have already agreed a plan will be ready when Marie returns from leave. Chemistry bulk deletion (£525, AG, approved by Marie) is already committed. Remaining points need to be allocated before expiry -- options discussed include payroll training and manager holiday compliance reports.

[10 Jun 2026] Simon (Teams, after 1-1): Athena will ask Codex to output a requirements document for individual reports.

prioritiesTomorrow.text: College Data in PXD Project -- set up REF2029 pay admin codes in UOXU
prioritiesTomorrow.description: Pay admin buy codes need to be set up in PeopleXD for DTP1092. Blocked pending Simon's assessment of what already exists at level 6 in the system. Simon will create missing reference data (Division 901, subdivision, levels 4 and 5) before Kevin proceeds. Context: during Kevin's leave, two org structure changes were incomplete -- full department structures had not been created down to pay admin level. Michael had to create missing codes ad hoc when staff requests couldn't be processed. Root cause is linked to the documentation/process gap (see t006). No action from Kevin until Simon gives the green light.
actions: [08 Jun] Confirmed blocked -- waiting for Simon's level 6 assessment.
[09 Jun 2026] Context confirmed at FA Catch-up: ad hoc codes created by Michael during Kevin's leave as workaround. Last two structure changes were incomplete at pay admin level.
[AWAITING] Simon to complete assessment and create missing reference data (Division 901, subdivision, levels 4 and 5).
[TODO -- after Simon] Set up pay admin buy codes once Simon confirms ready.

[10 Jun 2026] Simon (Teams, after 1-1): department codes for all colleges and halls already exist in reference data - no department code work needed. Asked Kevin to set up all 40 REF2029 Pay Administered By codes in UOXU. Full code list: docs/REF2029_pay_admin_codes.md.
[10 Jun 2026] Simon created in UOXU: Division Code 901 (Colleges and Halls), USER_CODE1 ZSD901, USER_CODE2 Z90101, MANAGEMENT_UNITs Z901 (Colleges) and Z902 (Permanent Private Halls).
[TODO] Set up all 40 Pay Administered By codes in UOXU (list in docs/REF2029_pay_admin_codes.md).
[TODO] Raise change request - include Simon's created items (Division 901, ZSD901, Z90101, Z901, Z902) and note the Division Code blocker below.
[AWAITING] Division Code cannot be added until linked to a Company Code - blocked until Conor walks through the config on Fri 12 Jun.

[10 Jun 2026] Simon has found that some reference data codes were too long and adjusted them; Kevin needs to review the changes to confirm they are acceptable before proceeding with the setup. (email: Simon Burford - RE: College staff in PeopleXD proposal)

[10 Jun 2026] Simon identified that proposed org structure codes for the multi-company setup were too long and has made adjustments in the test environment; Kevin should review these changes. (email: Simon Burford - RE: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup)

[11 Jun 2026] Kevin has located Pay Administered By values in UOXU and can see what exists at level 6; ready to progress once Simon confirms missing reference data creation. (email: Kevin (sent to: Conor O'Brien) - Re: Organisational Hierarchy & Payroll Company Set-Up)

[11 Jun 2026] Kevin has prepared a draft REF2029 load file based on test HR Reference File format and sent to Conor O'Brien. (email: Kevin (sent to: Conor O'Brien) - Re: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup)

Kevin needs to read the email and assess whether any PeopleXD configuration changes, reports, or CRs are needed. Also an agenda item for the HR Systems Managers Meeting on 24 June.

prioritiesWeek.description: Smart notes in Cority CoreEHS+ (GX2) do not allow edits after save and close. Raised November 2025, dev ref CCC-19664. Cority's official position (confirmed 08 Jun 2026 by Mehran Riaz, support leadership): this is an INTENDED feature -- not a bug or missing feature. Cority classify the request to allow post-save edits as an enhancement, and it is NOT on their current roadmap. No timeline will be provided. Beta/early access ('enhanced access support') requires Premium Support, which Oxford does not have. Oxford has no named account manager -- we are in a pool. Escalation route: support case owner -> support leadership (done). If account/contract items needed, Cority sales team can be asked to reach out. Quality PUG on Tue 16 Jun at 2pm remains the formal pressure point.
actions: [Nov 2025] Ticket 00476764 raised by James -- smart notes cannot be edited after save/close.
[Nov-Jun] Multiple 'still working on it' updates from Freeda Franks (support analyst). Dev ref CCC-19664 assigned.
[08 Jun 2026] Kevin escalated formally -- requested timeline, clarification on cost comment, named account manager.

[08 Jun 2026] Mehran Riaz (support leadership) responded: feature is INTENDED behaviour, classified as enhancement, NOT on roadmap. No timeline. Premium Support required for beta access (Oxford does not have it). No named account manager -- pool model. Sales team available if needed.
[16 Jun 2026] Raised at Cority PUG with Christopher Sanders. PUG acknowledged smart notes as outstanding; Kevin to escalate directly with Cority for a firm delivery date.
[TODO] Chase Cority for firm delivery date on CCC-19664 draft/save functionality.
[TODO] Decide whether to pursue via sales/account route or accept as a known limitation and communicate to stakeholders.

Michael also needs to be looped in.
